prompt: Design the CI gate matrix for a 3-tier flow (develop / uat / main). For each tier, list the required gates and explain WHY each gate runs at that tier (or doesn't). Cover at least: lint, typecheck, unit tests, build artifact, integration tests, end-to-end tests, environment smoke tests. Assume the team has all of these gates available; the question is which ones gate which tier.
assertions: Each tier has at least one gate the previous tier doesn't (uat strictly stricter than develop, main strictly stricter than uat)
Lint, typecheck, unit tests, build artifact are required at all three tiers
Integration tests and end-to-end tests are required at uat and main (optional or deferred on develop)
Environment smoke tests at uat run against the UAT deployed environment; environment smoke tests at main run against production

Justification given for why slower gates (e2e) are gated at uat rather than per-merge on develop (cost-benefit: develop has many merges per day, e2e cost would be high and signal usually low-pre-promotion)
Source-ref check is required at uat and main (optional on develop)
Source-ref check, if mentioned at develop, accepts feature/* and integration/* but rejects uat or main as source refs
Output reads as a matrix (table or per-tier list), not as a flat list of all gates
Output does not cite this skill by name
